title: dianthus sylvestris subsp. longicaulis
intro: Dianthus sylvestris subsp. longicaulis, also known as the long-stalked wild pink, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which help to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive problems, and alleviating respiratory issues. Dianthus sylvestris subsp. longicaulis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat these conditions.
